senses.gloss: to ram firmly, to thrust, to wedge
to ram firmly, to thrust, to wedge
wood peg, wedge, picket, stake, brad

examples.english: And [with] Pharaoh, owner of the stakes?

examples.text: فَدَخَلْتُ فَكَمَنْتُ، فَلَمَّا دَخَلَ النَّاسُ أَغْلَقَ الْبَابَ، ثُمَّ عَلَّقَ الأَغَالِيقَ عَلَى وَتَدٍ قَالَ فَقُمْتُ إِلَى الأَقَالِيدِ، فَأَخَذْتُهَا فَفَتَحْتُ الْبَابَ.
examples.english: So I went in (the castle) and hid myself. When the people got inside, the gate-keeper closed the gate and hung the keys on a fixed wooden peg. I got up and took the keys and opened the gate.

etymology.text: From the root و ت د (w t d). Cognate to Hebrew יָתֵד (yāted, “stake, peg”).
